description:
Create a Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/amenity_analysis.ipynb that calculates the non-residential square footage per capita for each zone and classifies highly populated residential zones by amenity access. Using the households, buildings, and parcels tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, map data to zone_ids and filter for zones where total_persons is at least 500. Calculate amenity_sqft_per_capita (total_non_residential_sqft / total_persons) and classify zones as 'Amenity Desert' (< 50 sqft per person), 'Moderate Access' (50 to < 200 sqft per person), or 'Amenity Rich' (>= 200 sqft per person). 

Export the results to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/amenity_deserts.csv including the zone_id, total_persons, total_non_residential_sqft, amenity_sqft_per_capita, and amenity_category. Generate a distribution chart of the categories at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/amenity_category_distribution.png. Save a summary JSON to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/amenity_summary.json with the keys: "total_amenity_deserts", "desert_population", and "highest_amenity_zone_id". Ensure all cells in the notebook are executed.
